text:
Top 15 Objectives of Recruitment and Selection

The article emphasizes that recruitment and selection transcend simply filling vacancies - they represent strategic investments in organizational success.

Key Recruitment Objectives: Identifying vacancies by analyzing organizational needs and job requirements; Attracting qualified candidates through compelling job descriptions and diverse recruitment channels; Building talent pools for future staffing needs; Enhancing organizational effectiveness through quality hiring decisions.

Selection Process Objectives: Assessing candidates' skills alignment with job requirements; Ensuring cultural compatibility with organizational values; Predicting future job performance; Reducing employee turnover through proper fit.

The Complete 15 Objectives: identifying vacancies, job analysis, attracting candidates, creating talent pools, increasing effectiveness, promoting employer brand, ensuring legal compliance, matching skills to roles, cultural alignment, performance prediction, reducing turnover, promoting diversity, improving quality of hire, legal compliance, and improving retention.

The article cites that 'organisations that invest in a strong candidate experience improve their quality of hires by 70%' and notes companies with strong recruitment processes achieved 'improved financial performance by 3x.'
